002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.microservice.tomcat;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.CollectionUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.FileUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.JsonMap.*;
026
027import java.io.*;
028import java.net.*;
029import java.nio.file.*;
030import java.nio.file.attribute.*;
031import java.time.*;
032import java.util.*;
033import java.util.concurrent.*;
034import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
035import java.util.logging.*;
036
037import org.apache.catalina.*;
038import org.apache.catalina.startup.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.config.event.*;
042import org.apache.juneau.marshall.cp.*;
043import org.apache.juneau.microservice.*;
044import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
045import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.*;
046import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.health.*;
047import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet.*;
048import org.apache.tomcat.util.descriptor.web.*;
049
050import jakarta.servlet.*;
051
052/**
053 * Drives the embedded Tomcat server lifecycle on behalf of the microservice via the {@link MicroserviceListener} fan-out.
054 *
055 * <p>
056 * Contributed by {@link TomcatConfiguration} as a <c>@Bean</c>.  The microservice's {@link Microservice#start()}
057 * call invokes {@link #onStart(Microservice)}, which:
058 * <ol>
059 *    <li>Picks the first available port from the resolved {@link TomcatSettings#getPorts() ports} (falling back to
060 *       <c>Tomcat/port</c> config, then <c>Tomcat-Port</c> manifest, then <c>8000</c>).
061 *    <li>Resolves the {@link Tomcat} instance — preferring an externally-supplied <c>@Bean Tomcat</c>, otherwise
062 *       building one programmatically via the {@link TomcatServerFactory} against a writable base directory.
063 *    <li>Mounts servlets from {@code Tomcat/servlets}, {@code Tomcat/servletMap}, {@code Tomcat/servletAttributes}
064 *       config sections, plus auto-mounts every {@code @Bean Servlet} whose runtime class is annotated with
065 *       {@link Rest @Rest} (at {@link Rest#path()}, or <c>"/"</c> when no path is set).
066 *    <li>Materializes the connector via {@link Tomcat#getConnector()}, starts the server, and publishes the bound
067 *       port via the {@code availablePort} / {@code juneau.serverPort} system properties.
068 * </ol>
069 *
070 * <p>
071 * {@link #onStop(Microservice)} performs the inverse: stops and destroys the server gracefully in a worker thread,
072 * then deletes the auto-created temp base directory (if one was created).
073 *
074 * <p>
075 * External callers reach this component via
076 * {@code Microservice.getInstance().getBeanStore().getBean(TomcatServerComponent.class).orElseThrow()}.
077 *
078 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
079 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauMicroserviceTomcatBasics">juneau-microservice-tomcat Basics</a>
080 * </ul>
081 *
082 * @since 10.0.0
083 */
084@SuppressWarnings({
085   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE convention (e.g., ROOT_CONTEXT_PATH)
086   "resource" // The Tomcat server is held by this component and closed in onStop(); its lifecycle spans onStart/onStop, so it cannot be try-with-resources. Eclipse JDT @Owning warning is by design.
087})
088public class TomcatServerComponent implements MicroserviceListener {
089
090   @SuppressWarnings("java:S2245") // Non-security use: port-selection jitter only; not used for tokens, session IDs, or any security-sensitive purpose.
091   private static final Random RANDOM = new Random();
092   private static final String ROOT_CONTEXT_PATH = "";
093   private static final String ROOT_DOC_BASE = ".";
094
095   /** Default bounded graceful-shutdown drain timeout applied when none is configured. */
096   static final Duration DEFAULT_STOP_TIMEOUT = Duration.ofSeconds(30);
097
098   private final Messages messages = Messages.of(TomcatServerComponent.class);
099   private final AtomicReference<Tomcat> tomcat = new AtomicReference<>();
100   private final AtomicReference<Microservice> microservice = new AtomicReference<>();
101   private final AtomicReference<File> baseDir = new AtomicReference<>();
102   private final AtomicBoolean ownsBaseDir = new AtomicBoolean(false);
103   private final AtomicReference<Duration> stopTimeout = new AtomicReference<>(DEFAULT_STOP_TIMEOUT);
104   private final AtomicReference<Duration> shutdownSettleDelay = new AtomicReference<>(Duration.ZERO);
105
106   /**
107    * Env-driven sentinel for {@code availablePort}; {@link Optional#empty()} when unset (in which case
108    * {@link #onStart(Microservice)} publishes the bound port back as the {@code availablePort} system property).
109    */
110   @Value("${availablePort}")
111   Optional<String> availablePortEnv = opte();
112
113   /**
114    * Env-driven sentinel for {@code juneau.serverPort}; {@link Optional#empty()} when unset (in which case
115    * {@link #onStart(Microservice)} publishes the bound port back as the {@code juneau.serverPort} system property).
116    */
117   @Value("${juneau.serverPort}")
118   Optional<String> serverPortEnv = opte();
119
120   private static int[] parseIntArray(String csv) {
121      if (e(csv))
122         return new int[0];
123      var parts = csv.split(",");
124      var out = new int[parts.length];
125      for (var i = 0; i < parts.length; i++)
126         out[i] = Integer.parseInt(parts[i].trim());
127      return out;
128   }
129
130   private static int findOpenPort(int[] ports) {
131      for (var port : ports) {
132         if (port == 0)
133            port = RANDOM.nextInt(32767 - ports[0] + 1) + ports[0];
134         try (var ss = new ServerSocket(port)) {
135            return port;
136         } catch (IOException e) {
137            // Port is in use, try next port in array
138         }
139      }
140      return 0;
141   }
142
143   private static String normalizePathSpec(String rawPath) {
144      var p = rawPath == null ? "" : rawPath;
145      if (p.isEmpty() || "/".equals(p))
146         return "/*";
147      if (! p.startsWith("/"))
148         p = "/" + p;
149      if (! p.endsWith("/*"))
150         p = trimTrailingSlashes(p) + "/*";
151      return p;
152   }
153
154   private static String normalizeExactPathSpec(String rawPath) {
155      var p = rawPath == null ? "" : trimTrailingSlashes(rawPath);
156      if (p.isEmpty())
157         return "/";
158      if (! p.startsWith("/"))
159         p = "/" + p;
160      return p;
161   }
162
163   /**
164    * Resolves the top-level Tomcat mount path-specs for an auto-discovered {@code @Rest}-annotated servlet.
165    *
166    * <p>
167    * Delegates to {@link RestContext#resolveTopLevelPaths(Class, Object, BeanStore)} so the runtime-override
168    * resolution chain (programmatic &gt; {@code getPaths()} getter &gt; annotation default) is honored at
169    * mount time. The programmatic Builder rung is N/A for Tomcat auto-discovery (the {@code RestContext} does
170    * not exist yet at this point in the lifecycle); users can substitute by overriding {@code getPaths()} on
171    * the resource class, or by leaning on SVL inside {@code @Rest(paths=...)} elements (e.g.
172    * {@code paths={"$C{health.paths}"}}) so each element is resolved through {@code Config} /
173    * {@code $E{...}} / {@code $S{...}} before being comma-split.
174    *
175    * @return The normalized exact-match path-specs (for multi-mount), or the singular {@code @Rest(path)}-style
176    *    {@code "/...&#47;*"} pathspec when no {@code paths} are resolved.
177    */
178   private static String[] restPathsFor(Servlet servlet, BeanStore store) {
179      var cls = servlet.getClass();
180      var r = cls.getAnnotation(Rest.class);
181      if (r == null)
182         return new String[] {"/*"};
183      var resolved = RestContext.resolveTopLevelPaths(cls, servlet, store);
184      if (resolved.length > 0)
185         return Arrays.stream(resolved).map(TomcatServerComponent::normalizeExactPathSpec).toArray(String[]::new);
186      return new String[] {normalizePathSpec(r.path())};
187   }
188
189   /**
190    * Constructor.
191    */
192   @SuppressWarnings({
193      "java:S1186" // Empty constructor; all state is initialized via onStart(Microservice) when wired through the bean store.
194   })
195   public TomcatServerComponent() {}
196
197   @Override /* Overridden from MicroserviceListener */
198   @SuppressWarnings({
199      "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for server creation logic
200   })
201   public void onStart(Microservice ms) {
202      try {
203         microservice.set(ms);
204         var store = ms.getBeanStore();
205         ClassInfo.of(this).inject(this, store);
206         var cf = ms.getConfig();
207         var mf = ms.getManifest();
208         var settings = store.getBean(TomcatSettings.class).orElseGet(() -> TomcatSettings.create().build());
209
210         // Port resolution: settings > Tomcat/port config > Tomcat-Port manifest > 8000
211         var ports = settings.getPorts();
212         if (ports == null)
213            ports = cf.get("Tomcat/port").as(int[].class)
214               .orElseGet(() -> mf.get("Tomcat-Port").map(TomcatServerComponent::parseIntArray).orElseGet(() -> ints(8000)));
215         var availablePort = findOpenPort(ports);
216
217         if (availablePortEnv.isEmpty())
218            System.setProperty("availablePort", String.valueOf(availablePort));
219
220         // Prefer a @Bean-supplied Tomcat, else build one programmatically via the factory.
221         var injectedTomcat = store.getBean(Tomcat.class).orElse(null);
222         if (nn(injectedTomcat)) {
223            tomcat.set(injectedTomcat);
224         } else {
225            var factory = store.getBean(TomcatServerFactory.class).orElseGet(BasicTomcatServerFactory::new);
226            tomcat.set(createServer(factory, resolveBaseDir(settings)));
227         }
228         tomcat.get().setPort(availablePort);
229         tomcat.get().getConnector();
230
231         // Publish back to the store for downstream beans / lookups.
232         store.addBean(Tomcat.class, tomcat.get());
233
234         // Graceful-shutdown wiring (zero-downtime k8s rollouts), kept identical to the Jetty contract:
235         //  - Resolve the bounded drain timeout (TomcatSettings > Tomcat/stopTimeout config > 30s default) so
236         //    onStop() can wait for in-flight requests to complete before the server stops.
237         //  - Remember the settle delay so onStop() can let the LB observe the /readyz 503 before draining.
238         //  - Mark readiness ready so a freshly-(re)started service serves traffic.
239         stopTimeout.set(firstNonNull(settings.getStopTimeout(), cf.get("Tomcat/stopTimeout").asLong().map(Duration::ofMillis).orElse(DEFAULT_STOP_TIMEOUT)));
240         shutdownSettleDelay.set(firstNonNull(settings.getShutdownSettleDelay(), cf.get("Tomcat/shutdownSettleDelay").asLong().map(Duration::ofMillis).orElse(Duration.ZERO)));
241         ReadinessState.resolve(store).markReady();
242
243         // Track each servlet pathSpec with its declaring source so we can fail loudly on collisions.
244         var mountedPaths = new LinkedHashMap<String,String>();
245
246         for (var s : cf.get("Tomcat/servlets").asStringArray().orElse(new String[0]))
247            mountConfiguredServlet(s, mountedPaths);
248
249         cf.get("Tomcat/servletMap").asMap().orElse(EMPTY_MAP).forEach((k, v) -> mountMappedServlet(k, v, mountedPaths));
250
251         cf.get("Tomcat/servletAttributes").asMap().orElse(EMPTY_MAP).forEach(this::addServletAttribute);
252
253         // Auto-mount @Bean AuthFilterChain at /* before any servlet registration so the filter
254         // runs before routing for all requests.
255         store.getBean(AuthFilterChain.class).ifPresent(chain -> addFilter(chain, "/*"));
256
257         // Auto-discover @Rest servlets contributed via @Configuration/@Bean methods.
258         // Path source precedence: @Rest(path=...) on the resource class, else "/".
259         for (var e : store.getBeansOfType(Servlet.class).entrySet()) {
260            var servlet = e.getValue();
261            var cls = servlet.getClass();
262            if (cls.getAnnotation(Rest.class) == null)
263               continue;
264            var pathSpecs = restPathsFor(servlet, store);
265            var source = "@Bean " + cls.getName() + (ne(e.getKey()) ? "[" + e.getKey() + "]" : "");
266            for (var pathSpec : pathSpecs)
267               checkPathCollision(pathSpec, source, mountedPaths);
268            addServlet(servlet, pathSpecs);
269         }
270
271         if (serverPortEnv.isEmpty())
272            System.setProperty("juneau.serverPort", String.valueOf(availablePort));
273
274         tomcat.get().start();
275         ms.out(messages, "ServerStarted", getPort());
276      } catch (Exception e) {
277         throw toRex(e);
278      }
279   }
280
281   @Override /* Overridden from MicroserviceListener */
282   public void onStop(Microservice ms) {
283      final Logger logger = ms.getLogger();
284      // Flip readiness out of service BEFORE the connector stops so /readyz returns 503 and the load balancer /
285      // Kubernetes stops routing new traffic while in-flight requests drain.  /livez stays healthy so the pod is
286      // not killed mid-drain.  The settle delay (default 0) gives the LB a window to observe the 503.
287      ReadinessState.resolve(ms.getBeanStore()).markOutOfService();
288      ms.out(messages, "DrainingRequests");
289      sleepQuietly(shutdownSettleDelay.get());
290      var t = new Thread("TomcatServerComponentStop") {
291         @Override /* Overridden from Thread */
292         public void run() {
293            try {
294               var t2 = tomcat.get();
295               if (t2 == null)
296                  return;
297               drainConnector(t2, stopTimeout.get(), logger);
298               ms.out(messages, "StoppingServer");
299               t2.stop();
300               t2.destroy();
301               ms.out(messages, "ServerStopped");
302            } catch (Exception e) {
303               logger.log(Level.WARNING, lm(e), e);
304            } finally {
305               cleanupBaseDir();
306            }
307         }
308      };
309      t.start();
310      try {
311         t.join();
312      } catch (InterruptedException e) {
313         Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
314      }
315   }
316
317   /**
318    * Pauses the connector (stops accepting new connections) and waits up to {@code timeout} for in-flight requests
319    * to drain before the caller stops the server.  Best-effort: never throws out of the shutdown sequence.
320    */
321   private static void drainConnector(Tomcat t2, Duration timeout, Logger logger) {
322      try {
323         var connector = t2.getConnector();
324         if (connector == null)
325            return;
326         connector.pause();
327         var executor = connector.getProtocolHandler() == null ? null : connector.getProtocolHandler().getExecutor();
328         if (executor instanceof ThreadPoolExecutor tpe) {
329            var deadline = System.nanoTime() + Math.max(0L, timeout.toNanos());
330            while (tpe.getActiveCount() > 0 && System.nanoTime() < deadline) {
331               if (sleepInterruptibly(50L))
332                  return;
333            }
334         }
335      } catch (Exception e) {
336         logger.log(Level.WARNING, lm(e), e);
337      }
338   }
339
340   private static void sleepQuietly(Duration d) {
341      if (d == null || d.isZero() || d.isNegative())
342         return;
343      try {
344         Thread.sleep(d.toMillis());
345      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") InterruptedException e) {
346         Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
347      }
348   }
349
350   private static boolean sleepInterruptibly(long millis) {
351      try {
352         Thread.sleep(millis);
353         return false;
354      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") InterruptedException e) {
355         Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
356         return true;
357      }
358   }
359
360   @Override /* Overridden from MicroserviceListener */
361   public void onConfigChange(Microservice ms, ConfigEvents events) {
362      // No-op: Intentional empty implementation - this component is purely lifecycle-driven.
363   }
364
365   /**
366    * Adds an arbitrary servlet to this Tomcat server.
367    *
368    * @param servlet The servlet instance.
369    * @param pathSpec The context path of the servlet.
370    * @return This object.
371    */
372   @SuppressWarnings("java:S3878") // Array wrapper required to dispatch to varargs overload; without it the call recurses infinitely back to this single-String overload.
373   public TomcatServerComponent addServlet(Servlet servlet, String pathSpec) {
374      return addServlet(servlet, new String[]{normalizePathSpec(pathSpec)});
375   }
376
377   /**
378    * Adds an arbitrary servlet to this Tomcat server at one or more context paths.
379    *
380    * @param servlet The servlet instance.
381    * @param pathSpecs The context paths of the servlet.
382    * @return This object.
383    */
384   public TomcatServerComponent addServlet(Servlet servlet, String...pathSpecs) {
385      var context = getContext();
386      var servletName = cn(servlet) + "#" + UUID.randomUUID();
387      Tomcat.addServlet(context, servletName, servlet);
388      for (var pathSpec : pathSpecs)
389         context.addServletMappingDecoded(pathSpec, servletName);
390      return this;
391   }
392
393   /**
394    * Adds a servlet filter to this Tomcat server at the specified URL pattern.
395    *
396    * @param filter The filter instance.
397    * @param urlPattern The URL pattern the filter applies to (e.g. {@code "/*"}, {@code "/api/*"}).
398    * @return This object.
399    */
400   @SuppressWarnings("java:S3878") // Array wrapper required to dispatch to varargs overload; without it the call recurses infinitely back to this single-String overload.
401   public TomcatServerComponent addFilter(jakarta.servlet.Filter filter, String urlPattern) {
402      return addFilter(filter, new String[]{urlPattern});
403   }
404
405   /**
406    * Adds a servlet filter to this Tomcat server at one or more URL patterns.
407    *
408    * @param filter The filter instance.
409    * @param urlPatterns The URL patterns the filter applies to.
410    * @return This object.
411    */
412   public TomcatServerComponent addFilter(jakarta.servlet.Filter filter, String...urlPatterns) {
413      var context = getContext();
414      var filterName = cn(filter) + "#" + UUID.randomUUID();
415      var fd = new FilterDef();
416      fd.setFilter(filter);
417      fd.setFilterName(filterName);
418      context.addFilterDef(fd);
419      for (var pattern : urlPatterns) {
420         var fm = new FilterMap();
421         fm.setFilterName(filterName);
422         fm.addURLPattern(pattern);
423         fm.setDispatcher(DispatcherType.REQUEST.name());
424         context.addFilterMap(fm);
425      }
426      return this;
427   }
428
429   /**
430    * Adds an attribute to the root servlet context of the Tomcat server.
431    *
432    * @param name The attribute name.
433    * @param value The attribute value.
434    * @return This object.
435    */
436   public TomcatServerComponent addServletAttribute(String name, Object value) {
437      getContext().getServletContext().setAttribute(name, value);
438      return this;
439   }
440
441   /**
442    * Returns the context path that this server is using.
443    *
444    * @return The context path.
445    */
446   public String getContextPath() {
447      return getContext().getPath();
448   }
449
450   /**
451    * Returns the root {@link Context} of the Tomcat server, creating it if necessary.
452    *
453    * @return The root context.
454    */
455   public Context getContext() {
456      var context = (Context)getServer().getHost().findChild(ROOT_CONTEXT_PATH);
457      if (context != null)
458         return context;
459      return getServer().addContext(ROOT_CONTEXT_PATH, ROOT_DOC_BASE);
460   }
461
462   /**
463    * Returns the hostname of this microservice.
464    *
465    * @return The hostname.
466    */
467   public String getHostName() {
468      String hostname = "localhost";
469      try {
470         hostname = InetAddress.getLocalHost().getHostName();
471      } catch (UnknownHostException e) {
472         // Cannot determine hostname, use default "localhost"
473      }
474      return hostname;
475   }
476
477   /**
478    * Returns the port that this server is listening on.
479    *
480    * @return The port.
481    */
482   public int getPort() {
483      return getServer().getConnector().getLocalPort();
484   }
485
486   /**
487    * Returns whether this server is using <js>"http"</js> or <js>"https"</js>.
488    *
489    * @return The protocol.
490    */
491   public String getProtocol() {
492      return getServer().getConnector().getSecure() ? "https" : "http";
493   }
494
495   /**
496    * Returns the underlying Tomcat server.
497    *
498    * @return The underlying Tomcat server.
499    * @throws IllegalStateException If the server has not been created yet.
500    */
501   public Tomcat getServer() {
502      return Objects.requireNonNull(tomcat.get(), "Tomcat not found.  Microservice has not been started yet.");
503   }
504
505   /**
506    * Returns the URI where this server is listening.
507    *
508    * @return The URI.
509    */
510   public URI getURI() {
511      var cp = getContextPath();
512      try {
513         return new URI(getProtocol(), null, getHostName(), getPort(), e(cp) ? null : cp, null, null);
514      } catch (URISyntaxException e) {
515         throw toRex(e);
516      }
517   }
518
519   private static Tomcat createServer(TomcatServerFactory factory, File dir) {
520      try {
521         return factory.create(dir);
522      } catch (Exception e) {
523         throw toRex(e);
524      }
525   }
526
527   private File resolveBaseDir(TomcatSettings settings) {
528      var configured = settings.getBaseDir();
529      if (ne(configured)) {
530         ownsBaseDir.set(false);
531         var f = new File(configured);
532         baseDir.set(f);
533         return f;
534      }
535      try {
536         var f = createTempBaseDir().toFile();
537         ownsBaseDir.set(true);
538         baseDir.set(f);
539         return f;
540      } catch (IOException e) {
541         throw toRex(e);
542      }
543   }
544
545   @SuppressWarnings({
546      "java:S5443" // Temp dir created via Files.createTempDirectory with owner-only (rwx------) POSIX perms; non-POSIX fallback relies on OS-default owner-restricted temp dirs.
547   })
548   private static Path createTempBaseDir() throws IOException {
549      try {
550         // Explicitly restrict to owner-only access (rwx------) on POSIX file systems.
551         Set<PosixFilePermission> ownerOnly = EnumSet.of(
552            PosixFilePermission.OWNER_READ,
553            PosixFilePermission.OWNER_WRITE,
554            PosixFilePermission.OWNER_EXECUTE);
555         return Files.createTempDirectory("juneau-tomcat",
556            PosixFilePermissions.asFileAttribute(ownerOnly));
557      } catch (UnsupportedOperationException e) {
558         // Non-POSIX filesystem (e.g. Windows) — fall back to default temp-dir creation.
559         return Files.createTempDirectory("juneau-tomcat");
560      }
561   }
562
563   private void cleanupBaseDir() {
564      if (ownsBaseDir.get()) {
565         var f = baseDir.getAndSet(null);
566         if (nn(f))
567            deleteFile(f);
568      }
569   }
570
571   private void mountConfiguredServlet(String className, Map<String,String> mountedPaths) {
572      try {
573         var c = info(Class.forName(className));
574         if (c.isAssignableTo(RestServlet.class)) {
575            var rs = (RestServlet)c.newInstance();
576            mountWithCollisionCheck(rs, rs.getPath(), "Tomcat/servlets[" + className + "]", mountedPaths);
577         } else {
578            throw rex("Invalid servlet specified in Tomcat/servlets.  Must be a subclass of RestServlet: {0}", className);
579         }
580      } catch (ClassNotFoundException e) {
581         throw toRex(e);
582      }
583   }
584
585   private void mountMappedServlet(String pathKey, Object className, Map<String,String> mountedPaths) {
586      try {
587         var c = info(Class.forName(className.toString()));
588         if (c.isAssignableTo(Servlet.class)) {
589            var rs = (Servlet)c.newInstance();
590            mountWithCollisionCheck(rs, pathKey, "Tomcat/servletMap[" + pathKey + "]", mountedPaths);
591         } else {
592            throw rex("Invalid servlet specified in Tomcat/servletMap.  Must be a subclass of Servlet: {0}", cn(className));
593         }
594      } catch (ClassNotFoundException e) {
595         throw toRex(e);
596      }
597   }
598
599   private void mountWithCollisionCheck(Servlet servlet, String rawPath, String source, Map<String,String> mountedPaths) {
600      var pathSpec = normalizePathSpec(rawPath);
601      checkPathCollision(pathSpec, source, mountedPaths);
602      addServlet(servlet, pathSpec);
603   }
604
605   private void checkPathCollision(String pathSpec, String source, Map<String,String> mountedPaths) {
606      var prior = mountedPaths.get(pathSpec);
607      if (nn(prior))
608         throw rex("Servlet mount path collision: ''{0}'' is already mounted by {1}; refused by {2}.", pathSpec, prior, source);
609      mountedPaths.put(pathSpec, source);
610   }
611}
